digest.component_or_area: release pipeline & Hatchling provenance build hook
propositions: The release build job and CI-tested code come from different build paths, so the published artifact is never install- or functionally tested before publish.
The default python -m build path builds the wheel from the extracted sdist which lacks .git, so the published wheel bakes COMMIT = None while only the sdist retains the real SHA.
The provenance hook's initialize unconditionally rewrites _build_info.py with no preservation of an existing baked commit and no failure when the commit is absent.

Artifacts should be built once, upload a single bundle with recorded SHA-256 sums, be install-tested in isolated wheel and sdist jobs, and published byte-for-byte after hash verification with no rebuild.

The hook must resolve the commit by precedence: REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT env var (short SHA), then preserve an existing non-null COMMIT, then git rev-parse --short HEAD, else None, failing only when the env var is set but unresolvable.
_build_info.py must be added to force_include so the baked SHA ships in the sdist, enabling the preserve-existing path to retain provenance through pip install of the sdist.
